rationale: Leg-9.5 correctness lens: git diff 4dfebbcb a2f2451 -- internal/panel/gate.go is COMMENT-ONLY (one word, 'byte-superset' -> 'substring-set superset'); tally.go is untouched (empty diff). Leg 9.5 / UnresolvedVerdicts / VoteDecision are byte-identical to the round-1-approved code. E2e-assertion lens: the new 'unresolved REQUEST_CHANGES' assertion matches the real leg-9.5 message format verbatim (fmt.Sprintf literal), so the test PASSES on real code (verified green in /tmp copy). It genuinely pins the block to leg 9.5: under the leg-9.5-disabled mutation, panel_gate_e2e_test.go:226 (this exact assertion) is what fires. The panel_decision revA/revB rename also discriminates: mutation makes the two-RC row report 'message missing revA'/'revB' (single letters x/y were substrings of fixed Block text; revA/revB enter only via strings.Join slot %s, none appear as fixed text in gate.go). Full build/gofmt/vet/test green in /tmp; shared worktree clean at a2f2451.

findings.note: gate.go delta is a single-word comment reword; tally.go byte-identical (empty diff). No production logic changed.

Leg-9.5 message literal contains 'unresolved REQUEST_CHANGES'; e2e assertion matches (real code PASSES). Mutation-verified: leg-9.5-disabled makes the new assertion at panel_gate_e2e_test.go:226 fire, and the two-RC panel_decision row fail on missing revA/revB. revA/revB never appear as fixed substrings in the message format.
